description_long: This sculpture depicts a classical, partially draped female figure standing on a rectangular plinth. The figure is missing both arms and the head, with the left arm broken off above the elbow and the right arm at the shoulder. The drapery is intricately carved, wrapping around the hips and falling in naturalistic folds, leaving the upper torso and one leg exposed. The feet are bare, with the left foot slightly forward, suggesting a gentle contrapposto stance. The surface has a weathered, stone-like appearance with subtle variations in tone, evoking the look of ancient marble. The overall form and style are reminiscent of Greco-Roman statuary.
description: A weathered, classical-style female torso sculpture with missing arms and head, draped in detailed fabric and standing on a rectangular base.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the exposed torso, missing head and arms, and detailed drapery over the hips and legs.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the drapery and the forward placement of the left foot.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back, the way the drapery wraps around, and the absence of detail on the back of the head and arms.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizing the curvature of the back, the right leg, and the draped fabric's flow.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific environment.
